title: Oracle Releases Emergency Out-of-Band Patch for Critical CVE-2026-21992 (CVSS 9.8): Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager via Missing Authentication in REST WebServices
affected_products: Oracle Web Services Manager versions 12.2.1.4.0 and 14.1.2.1.0
Oracle Fusion Middleware deployments using affected Identity Manager or Web Services Manager components

sources.name: BleepingComputer: Oracle Pushes Emergency Fix for Critical Identity Manager RCE Flaw
The Hacker News: Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
Security Affairs: Oracle Fixes Critical RCE Flaw CVE-2026-21992 in Identity Manager
Tenable: CVE-2026-21992 Critical Out-of-Band Oracle Identity Manager and Web Services Manager
Help Net Security: Oracle Issues Emergency Fix for Pre-Auth RCE in Identity Manager (CVE-2026-21992)
Oracle Security Blog: Security Alert CVE-2026-21992 Released
Dark Reading: Patch Now - Oracle's Fusion Middleware Has Critical RCE Flaw
CyberPress: Oracle Releases Urgent Patch for Critical RCE Flaw in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager

timeline.event: Related vulnerability CVE-2025-61757 in Oracle Identity Manager REST WebServices was exploited in the wild and added to CISA KEV catalog, establishing this component as a known attack target

Oracle releases emergency out-of-band security alert for CVE-2026-21992, a critical unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager with CVSS 9.8, outside the regular quarterly patch cycle
Oracle strongly recommends immediate patching; multiple security outlets including BleepingComputer, The Hacker News, Dark Reading, and Tenable publish alerts
No active exploitation confirmed yet, but the precedent of CVE-2025-61757 (same component) being exploited in the wild makes weaponization highly likely given the low attack complexity

simple_summary.what_happened: Oracle released an emergency out-of-band security patch on March 23, 2026 - outside its regular quarterly patch cycle - to fix a critical vulnerability in Oracle Identity Manager and Oracle Web Services Manager. The flaw allows anyone on the network to remotely execute code on the server over plain HTTP without needing to log in or have any credentials. Oracle considers this serious enough to warrant an emergency advisory, strongly urging immediate patching. A nearly identical vulnerability in the same component was actively exploited in the wild just months ago.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Organizations running Oracle Identity Manager versions 12.2.1.4.0 or 14.1.2.1.0, or Oracle Web Services Manager versions 12.2.1.4.0 or 14.1.2.1.0. Oracle Identity Manager is a core enterprise identity and access management (IAM) platform used for managing user accounts, permissions, and access across enterprise applications. It is widely deployed in financial services, government, healthcare, and large enterprises for identity governance and compliance.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attacker identifies Oracle Identity Manager or Web Services Manager instances accessible over the network, targeting the REST WebServices component on standard HTTP ports. Step 2: Attacker sends a crafted HTTP request to the REST WebServices endpoint - no authentication, credentials, or user interaction is required. Step 3: The server processes the request due to missing authentication on a critical function, allowing the attacker's payload to execute as arbitrary code on the server. Step 4: Attacker gains remote code execution on the Identity Manager server, with access to the identity database containing all user accounts, permissions, and access policies across the enterprise. Step 5: Attacker can create backdoor admin accounts, modify access policies, extract user credentials, and pivot to any system managed by the compromised Identity Manager deployment.
what_you_should_do: Apply the Oracle out-of-band security patch for CVE-2026-21992 immediately - do not wait for the next quarterly patch cycle
Restrict network access to Oracle Identity Manager and Web Services Manager REST WebServices endpoints to trusted administrative networks only
Audit Oracle Identity Manager for any unauthorized account creation, permission changes, or policy modifications since the vulnerability became exploitable
Monitor the CISA KEV catalog for potential addition of CVE-2026-21992 given the precedent of CVE-2025-61757 exploitation in the same component
Review Oracle Identity Manager access logs for any unusual REST API calls from unexpected source IPs or at unusual hours
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: Oracle Identity Manager is the enterprise backbone for managing who has access to what. A compromised Identity Manager gives an attacker the ability to create admin accounts, grant themselves access to any system, and modify access policies across the entire organization - effectively becoming an invisible super-administrator. The fact that Oracle issued an emergency out-of-band patch (rare for Oracle) signals the severity. The precedent of CVE-2025-61757 in the same REST WebServices component being exploited in the wild just months ago makes rapid weaponization of this new flaw highly probable.

tactics.description: Attackers exploit the missing authentication in Oracle Identity Manager REST WebServices endpoint to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution over HTTP
Arbitrary code execution on the Oracle Identity Manager server through the unauthenticated REST WebServices vulnerability
Attacker can use compromised Identity Manager to create backdoor administrative accounts across all managed enterprise applications and systems
Attacker modifies access policies and grants elevated permissions through the compromised Identity Manager, obtaining legitimate admin credentials for managed systems
Access to the Identity Manager database allows extraction of user credentials, password hashes, and authentication tokens for all managed user accounts

detection_guidance.summary: Detection should focus on monitoring HTTP requests to Oracle Identity Manager and Web Services Manager REST API endpoints, with particular attention to unauthenticated requests and unusual administrative actions. Given the precedent of exploitation in the same component, proactive threat hunting is strongly recommended.

log_sources.description: Monitor for unauthenticated REST API calls, unusual account creation, permission modifications, and policy changes that may indicate exploitation or post-exploitation activity

log_sources.source: Web Application Firewall (WAF) / Load Balancer Logs
log_sources.description: Monitor HTTP requests to Identity Manager and Web Services Manager endpoints for requests bypassing authentication from unexpected source IPs
Alert on HTTP traffic to Oracle Identity Manager REST API ports from external or untrusted network segments
Correlate Identity Manager events with downstream system access patterns to detect unauthorized account usage across managed applications

what_to_look_for.indicator: Unauthenticated HTTP requests to Oracle Identity Manager REST WebServices endpoints
what_to_look_for.description: CVE-2026-21992 exploitation involves unauthenticated HTTP requests to the REST WebServices component; any request without valid authentication tokens is suspicious

what_to_look_for.indicator: Unexpected administrative account creation or permission changes in Identity Manager
what_to_look_for.description: Post-exploitation may involve creating backdoor admin accounts or modifying access policies; alert on any account creation or role assignments outside change management windows
what_to_look_for.log_field: action_type IN ('CREATE_USER', 'ASSIGN_ROLE', 'MODIFY_POLICY') AND source NOT IN approved_admin_systems

what_to_look_for.indicator: REST API calls from unexpected source IP addresses or at unusual hours
what_to_look_for.description: Exploitation originates from attacker infrastructure; monitor for REST API access from IPs not in the authorized management network range, especially outside business hours
what_to_look_for.log_field: src_ip NOT IN oim_admin_network AND api_endpoint CONTAINS 'rest' AND time NOT IN business_hours

what_to_look_for.indicator: Process execution anomalies on Oracle Identity Manager server
what_to_look_for.description: Successful RCE may spawn unexpected processes on the Identity Manager server; monitor for unusual child processes from the WebLogic or Identity Manager JVM process
what_to_look_for.log_field: parent_process CONTAINS 'java' AND parent_cmdline CONTAINS 'weblogic' AND child_process NOT IN expected_processes

immediate_actions.action: Apply Oracle out-of-band security patch for CVE-2026-21992 immediately
immediate_actions.description: Oracle released the emergency patch on March 23, 2026. Apply to all Oracle Identity Manager (12.2.1.4.0, 14.1.2.1.0) and Web Services Manager (12.2.1.4.0, 14.1.2.1.0) deployments. Do not wait for the next quarterly patch cycle.

immediate_actions.action: Restrict network access to Identity Manager REST WebServices endpoints
immediate_actions.description: Immediately implement firewall rules and ACLs to restrict access to Oracle Identity Manager REST API endpoints to authorized management networks only. Block all external and untrusted network access.

immediate_actions.action: Audit Identity Manager for unauthorized changes since vulnerability became exploitable
immediate_actions.description: Review all account creation, role assignments, permission changes, and policy modifications in Oracle Identity Manager. Compare against change management records to identify any unauthorized modifications.

immediate_actions.action: Monitor for CISA KEV addition and potential active exploitation announcements
immediate_actions.description: Given that CVE-2025-61757 in the same component was added to CISA KEV after active exploitation was confirmed, closely monitor threat intelligence feeds for evidence of CVE-2026-21992 exploitation in the wild.
